title: Secure OS command path searching
description: Searching for OS commands in the system PATH can be exploited if attackers can manipulate the PATH environment variable or place malicious executables in PATH directories. This can lead to execution of unintended commands.
recommendation: Use absolute paths for critical commands or validate the PATH environment before command execution.

noncompliant.description: Relying on PATH for command resolution allows attackers to execute malicious commands if they control PATH or place executables in PATH directories.

compliant.description: Using absolute paths, command whitelists, and path validation prevents execution of unintended commands.

checks.message: Avoid relying on PATH for command resolution - use absolute paths
